body.title: [pending] Revive the cross-day watch-for primitive — Akien asks CC to watch something, CC surfaces it later
body.description:
intention: I intend that when Akien asks CC to keep an eye on something, CC surfaces it back to him later without being reminded of the topic — and that watches expire by default, so the list stays short enough to actually read.
why: A memory is passive recall; it surfaces only when the topic recurs, which is exactly the case where Akien did not need CC watching. A watch must fire whether or not the topic recurs. And an accumulating watch list is noise nobody reads — which is how this primitive died the first time (closed done, never built).

Akien (2026-07-10): he can use CC's memory as HIS memory — "keep an eye on X and come back to me about it later." Distinct from remembering-for-CC: a memory is passive recall (surfaces only when the topic recurs); a watch must FIRE whether or not the topic recurs.

The primitive was already specified and closed done. It does not exist.
  - T-audit-day-skill (closed `done`, pri 0.5, 2026-04-29) specifies: "cross-day watch-for (yesterday's notes -- fire if hit, age if not, expire if stale)".
  - skills/audit-day/SKILL.md Step 3 documents it and calls `read_watch_next` / `emit_watch_next`.
  - `devlab/claudecode/audit_telemetry.py` DOES NOT EXIST anywhere in the repo. The skill imports a phantom module.
  - `/audit-day` is invoked by nothing. `/day-close` step 4 runs `/day-close-audit`, which has zero watch references.
A hollow build closed `done`, predating proof-on-close (gate 2026-06-20 vs ticket 2026-04-29). Retroactive-proof candidate: the class is exactly what the gate exists to catch.

Akien's binding constraint: "i don't think we wanna keep a lot of those, because a lot of them would be noise." Scarcity is the DESIGN, not a caveat. The default fate of a watch is to expire unread; only repeat-hitters survive. An accumulating watch list is how this died the first time.

Two kinds of watch; only one is probeable:
  - PROBEABLE ("does file X still exist", "is the unproven count still climbing") -> already served by `validity_conditions` + validity_sweep.py, which runs every day-close and is STARVED (0/130 proofs, 14/2734 records carry conditions). Do not build a second mechanism for this.
  - OBSERVATIONAL ("am I still making this mistake") -> no probe can resolve it; it only accrues when CC notices an instance mid-work. This has no home. It is the kind Akien actually asked for.

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/audit_telemetry.py (create), skills/day-close-audit/SKILL.md (add the watch step to the path that ACTUALLY runs), skills/audit-day/SKILL.md (reconcile or retire -- it is orphaned), devlab/runtime/memory/notes/ (watch record home, TBD vs a new watches/ category -- ASK AKIEN before creating a new store category)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite (flat-file JSON in the store); canonical-memory-home (watch records live under devlab/runtime/memory/, nowhere else); proof-on-close; calm-signals (a watch surfacing is a list, never an urgency flag); no-silent-caps (if the watch list is capped, log what was dropped)
**Scope boundary:** IN -- emit_watch/read_watch with ttl_days, fire/age/expire semantics, a hard cap on active watches, wiring into the day-close path that actually runs, and surfacing hits TO AKIEN (not annotating in place like validity_sweep does). OUT -- rebuilding probeable watches (use validity_conditions); OUT -- any auto-action on a hit; a watch reports, it never decides. OUT -- reviving /audit-day as a separate skill unless the reconcile step says so.
**Test plan:** proof-on-close via mutation-red. Property: a watch emitted with ttl_days=N is returned by read_watch before N days and marked expired after; a watch that hits is reported and its age resets. Mutation: break the TTL comparison in the CURRENT tree -> the test must fail. Second node: an expired watch is NOT surfaced (guards the noise constraint -- this is the assertion that encodes Akien's "don't keep a lot of those"). Hermetic: monkeypatch the store root to tmp_path, never touch devlab/runtime/memory/ (see T-proof-emitter-tests-pollute-store).

**Why it matters:** the watch primitive is the execution half of [[project_two_memories_lean_against_each_other]] -- Akien's pattern-sense points, CC's corpus confirms. Without an active watch, CC only ever confirms what Akien already thought to ask about.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

**Completion criteria:**
- devlab/claudecode/audit_telemetry.py exists and exports emit_watch / read_watch with ttl_days.
- A watch past its TTL is NOT returned by read_watch (test asserts this).
- The day-close path that ACTUALLY runs (/day-close-audit) surfaces watch hits.
- /audit-day is wired in or explicitly retired — no orphaned skill importing a phantom module.
- A hard cap on active watches exists, and dropping any is logged (no silent caps).
- Proof node passes mutation-red: break the TTL comparison in the current tree, test goes red.
